programmes.show_description: 1/2. It's a future where computers rule and artificial intelligence is a reality. Stars Alphonsia Emmanuel and Mona Hammond.
4 Extra Debut. 1960s. A lecturer must re-live childhood memories of a murder. Did he contribute to a miscarriage of justice? Stars Rob Edwards.
After the war, the unsung story of the women who worked in the early American intelligence agency. Read by Nicola Stuart-Hill.
Fosdyke International is going into red cabbage, pigs' feet and maybe even pickled onions. Starring Philip Lowrie. From 1983.
1/6. The shady lawyer stays in a haunted mansion. The Marx Brothers radio shows recreated. With guest Spike Milligan. From July 1992.
6/20. Desert island doings for the 'Swiss Family Horne' - and 'Hornerama' probes the press. Stars Kenneth Horne. From November 1960.
3/3. Charting the highs and lows of becoming a mother, Jessica Fostekew tackles coping with the new arrival.
4 Extra Debut. From Bach to Gloria Gaynor. Oscar-winning costume designer Jenny Beavan shares her music choices with Lauren Laverne. From 2023.
The famous naturalist looks at why underground animals evolved in a very different way from those on the surface. From 2009.
Daljit Nagra selects The Psalms read by actor Jeremy Irons featuring some of the most beautiful poetry in the Bible.  From 2019.
...Mole, Aged 13 3/4 (Omnibus). 1/2. It's 1981 and the year starts inauspiciously for Adrian Mole. A new girl called Pandora joins his class. Read by Harry McEntire.
Actor Mark Heap chooses 'Were You There?' by Tennessee Ernie Ford and 'L'Ascension' composed by Olivier Messiaen.
And in a packed programme, a future comedy star is born opposite a fire station. Ronnie Corbett reads from his autobiography.
